result.stdout:
Hybridizer Essentials (c) 2025 - 7.0.2.0

Hybridizer transpiles dotnet assemblies to native code. It can run in two modes (when fully licensed):
    --help : this message
    --display-license-details: show license details
    ## JAVA MODE : 
        --java : generate JAVA source wrapping the native code generated beforehand. If set the following must be and others will be ignored :
            --java-output-directory : where to generate JAVA source
            --dll-fullpaths (required) : ';' separated list of assemblies
            --flavors, -f (required) : flavors (CUDA, OMP) to generate JAVA for, ';' separated
            --default-java-package : name for the root package
    ## HYBRIDIZE MODE : 
        --dll-fullpaths (required) : ';' separated list of assemblies to hybridize
        --flavors, -f (required) : flavors (CUDA, OMP, HIP, AVX, AVX2, AVX512) to generate, ';' separated
        --working-directory, -cwd (required) : where to generate source code
        --pdb-fullpaths: ';' separated list of debug symbols
        --license : license file path or license string
        --generate-line-info, -g : interleave #line debug info in generated source code
        --use-function-pointers, -ufp : generate function pointers in dispatch file
        --use-hybrid-arrays, -uha : use hybrid arrays (bound checked) instead of raw pointers, comes at a cost
        --builtins : builtins files, ';' separated list
        --lines-per-file : max lines per generated files (useful on windows for very large projects where nvcc can't handle too much code per compilation unit)
    ## BASIC MODE
    Hybridizer Free edition can use two additional options :
        --jit-compil-options (basic only) : additional options for nvrtc JITTER
        --additional-jit-headers (basic only) : additional headers for nvrtc <file_name:directory_path> pairs, ';' separated. Paths are relative to cwd.
        --jit-cuda-version (13.0|13.1) : version of CUDA runtime and driver to use

Hybridizer Essentials (c) 2025 - 7.0.2.0

Hybridizer transpiles dotnet assemblies to native code. It can run in two modes (when fully licensed):
    --help : this message
    --display-license-details: show license details
    ## JAVA MODE : 
        --java : generate JAVA source wrapping the native code generated beforehand. If set the following must be and others will be ignored :
            --java-output-directory : where to generate JAVA source
            --dll-fullpaths (required) : ';' separated list of assemblies
            --flavors, -f (required) : flavors (CUDA, OMP) to generate JAVA for, ';' separated
            --default-java-package : name for the root package
    ## HYBRIDIZE MODE : 
        --dll-fullpaths (required) : ';' separated list of assemblies to hybridize
        --flavors, -f (required) : flavors (CUDA, OMP, HIP, AVX, AVX2, AVX512) to generate, ';' separated
        --working-directory, -cwd (required) : where to generate source code
        --pdb-fullpaths: ';' separated list of debug symbols
        --license : license file path or license string
        --generate-line-info, -g : interleave #line debug info in generated source code
        --use-function-pointers, -ufp : generate function pointers in dispatch file
        --use-hybrid-arrays, -uha : use hybrid arrays (bound checked) instead of raw pointers, comes at a cost
        --builtins : builtins files, ';' separated list
        --lines-per-file : max lines per generated files (useful on windows for very large projects where nvcc can't handle too much code per compilation unit)
    ## BASIC MODE
    Hybridizer Free edition can use two additional options :
